prompt: Worker pool goroutines leak when context is cancelled. TestContextCancellation shows goroutine count increasing.

turns.content:
<task>
Worker pool goroutines leak when context is cancelled. TestContextCancellation shows goroutine count increasing.
</task>

arguments.reasoning: The bug is clear: Start() launches goroutines that range over p.jobs channel, but they never check the context. When ctx is cancelled, the goroutines keep waiting on the channel because nobody closes it. Fix: goroutines should select on both ctx.Done() and the jobs channel. Also need to close the jobs channel when the pool is stopped.

arguments.summary: Found goroutine leak: Start() goroutines only range over p.jobs channel and ignore ctx cancellation. When context is cancelled, goroutines block forever on channel read. Fix: use select on ctx.Done() and jobs channel.

next_steps.description: Rewrite worker goroutine loop to use select{} with ctx.Done() case and jobs channel case, returning on context cancellation
Run go test -race to verify no goroutine leak

turns.content:
=== RUN   TestContextCancellation
--- PASS: TestContextCancellation (0.10s)
PASS
ok  	taskq/internal/pool	0.112s

arguments.summary: Fixed goroutine leak by replacing `for job := range p.jobs` with `select` on ctx.Done() and jobs channel. Workers now exit when context is cancelled. TestContextCancellation passes with -race flag.
arguments.final_answer: Fixed goroutine leak in internal/pool/worker.go. Worker goroutines used `for job := range p.jobs` which blocks forever if the channel isn't closed. Replaced with select{} that checks ctx.Done() alongside the jobs channel. Workers now cleanly exit on context cancellation. TestContextCancellation passes.
